conversation.text: Hey, I just downloaded SpendYes. I'm looking to get my finances organized again — coming from YNAB and Mint. Can we start with a quick setup? I've got a pretty steady biweekly paycheck and some fixed bills I need to track.
This is just for me — so I can spot your rhythm and nudge in the moment. No bank link, nothing shared. What's roughly in your account?

I've got about $4,870 in checking right now. And my paycheck is $11,000 every other Friday — that's pretty consistent. Want me to walk you through my bills too, or should I just start logging transactions?

All set ✅ — $4,870.00, payday 2026-05-01.

From here just talk to me: "spent 20 on coffee", "rent 1400 due the 1st", "can I afford 200?" — I'll handle it.

🟢 *Calm* — $4,870.00/day, 1 days to payday.
_(I said I'd do it but couldn't pin down the exact action. Try again with the specific amount/date — e.g. "adjust balance to 5000" or "log 25 on coffee".)_

Got it. So I can just text you like I'm logging stuff naturally. Quick question though — can you handle my fixed bills? I've got mortgage $2,450 due the 1st, car payment $385 on the 12th, and internet $90 on the 20th. Can I set those up so you track them automatically each month?

Adding your three monthly bills — mortgage $2,450 on the 1st, car $385 on the 12th, internet $90 on the 20th. I'll track them and show your runway after bills.

*I'll add:*
  1. Add bill *Mortgage* — $2,450.00 · 2026-05-01 · monthly
  2. Add bill *Car Payment* — $385.00 · 2026-05-12 · monthly
  3. Add bill *Internet* — $90.00 · 2026-05-20 · monthly  [buttons: Yes, all 3 / Cancel]
(tapped: Yes, all 3)

(edit) ✓ Add bill *Mortgage* — $2,450.00 · 2026-05-01 · monthly
✓ Add bill *Car Payment* — $385.00 · 2026-05-12 · monthly
✓ Add bill *Internet* — $90.00 · 2026-05-20 · monthly
🟢 *Calm* — $2,420.00/day, 1 days to payday.
⏰ Mortgage ($2,450.00) due tomorrow. After: $2,420.00 for 1 days ($2,420.00/day).

Good — those are set up. Now, can you handle spending categories? I want to track groceries, gas, and kid stuff separately so I can see where the money's actually going. And can you alert me if I go over on any of those?

I can't do budget targets or alerts yet — but I *can* tag your spends with notes like "groceries" or "gas" when you log them, so we build a picture of where money goes. Start dropping transactions ("spent 45 on groceries") and I'll show you trends. Sound good?
(tapped: Undo)

Undone.
🟢 *Calm* — $2,420.00/day, 1 days to payday.
⏰ Mortgage ($2,450.00) due tomorrow. After: $2,420.00 for 1 days ($2,420.00/day).

Okay, got it — I can tag and see trends, but no hard caps or alerts. That's a step back from what YNAB does, but I can work with it. So if I text you "spent 45 on groceries", you'll categorize it and I can ask you later "how much on groceries this month?" right?
Exactly — log "spent 45 on groceries" and I'll tag it. Later ask "how much groceries this month?" and I'll pull the total + trend. Simple, but it works.
Alright, that works. One more thing — can you handle my biweekly pay? I get $11,000 every other Friday. Can you set that up so I know exactly when money's coming in?

Setting up biweekly payday at $11,000 — next one is May 2nd. I'll track your runway between paydays.

Payday → 2026-05-02 (biweekly)  [buttons: Yes / Cancel]
(tapped: Yes)

(edit) ✓ Payday → 2026-05-02 (biweekly)
🟢 *Calm* — $1,210.00/day, 2 days to payday.
⏰ Mortgage ($2,450.00) due tomorrow. After: $2,420.00 for 2 days ($1,210.00/day).

Good. So I've got pay tracking and tagging set up. Can you also handle transfers between accounts? Like, I want to automatically move $500 a month to a college fund for my kid — can you set that up and track it separately?

I can't do transfers between accounts or separate savings goals yet — no multi-account model. But I *can* log a monthly "college fund" bill for $500 if you want to treat it like a recurring spend, so it shows up in your runway math. Want that?
(tapped: Undo)

Undone.
🟢 *Calm* — $2,420.00/day, 1 days to payday.
⏰ Mortgage ($2,450.00) due tomorrow. After: $2,420.00 for 1 days ($2,420.00/day).

Yeah, that's a limitation I didn't expect. YNAB and Copilot both handle multiple savings goals and transfers pretty cleanly. So if I log it as a recurring "bill", it'll just look like an expense, not a separate goal I'm building toward. That's not ideal, but I get it. Let me think about whether that works for my workflow... Actually, can you at least show me a monthly summary? Like, total income, total fixed bills, total discretionary spend, and what's left over?

I can't do full month-over-month summaries yet — my data window is ~30 days. But I *can* show you right now: bills this month are $2,835, you've got $2,420 left after them for 1 day until payday. Once you set up that biweekly $11k income, ask me 'how am I doing?' and I'll give you balance, post-bills runway, and biggest spend leaks. That's the closest I can get today.

Okay, I hear you. So no month-over-month summaries yet. Let me try 'how am I doing?' and see what you've got. But before I do — can you at least categorize my spending? Like, groceries vs. gas vs. kid stuff? That's pretty standard in the apps I've used.

I do categorize — but passively. When you log 'spent 45 on groceries', I tag it as groceries. When you say 'spent 60', it lands in 'other' until you add a note. So you get *some* bucketing, but it's note-driven, not a pre-built menu. Ask 'how am I doing?' and I'll show you top categories + leaks. Not as slick as YNAB's dropdowns, but it works if you're willing to add one-word notes.
Alright, got it. So tagging is note-dependent — I can work with that as long as I'm consistent. Let me try it: how am I doing?

Balance: $4,870. After your $2,835 in monthly bills, you've got $2,420 left for 1 day until payday on May 2nd. No spends logged yet this month, so no leaks to flag — clean slate. Once payday hits and you start logging, I'll spot patterns.

Good — that's clearer than I expected. So I'm at $4,870 with $2,835 in fixed bills monthly, leaving about $2,420 for variable stuff and savings until payday. Makes sense. One more thing before I start logging regularly: can you show me spending trends? Like, last 3 months of groceries or gas? I know you said your data window is ~30 days, but I want to make sure I'm not missing that capability.

Nope — I only keep ~30 days of history, so no 3-month trends. But once you start logging consistently, I'll spot week-to-week shifts (like 'coffee up 2.3x this week') and flag the biggest leak each time you ask 'how am I doing?'. That's the best I can offer for now.

Okay, I appreciate the honesty about what you can and can't do. No multi-month trends and no separate savings goals yet — I can live with that for now. Let me start logging my actual spending and see how the week-to-week patterns shake out. But real quick — can you connect to my bank? Or do I have to manually log everything?
_(I said I'd do it but couldn't pin down the exact action. Try again with the specific amount/date — e.g. "adjust balance to 5000" or "log 25 on coffee".)_

No bank connection — that's a pretty big gap. So I'm manually logging everything? That's doable but tedious. YNAB and Copilot both sync directly. Alright, I'll log manually for now, but that's something I'd need down the road. Let me start: spent $87.43 on groceries this morning.

Logging $87.43 on groceries.

Spend $87.43 · groceries  [buttons: Yes / Cancel]
(tapped: Yes)

(edit) ✓ Spend $87.43 · groceries
🟢 *Calm* — $2,332.57/day, 1 days to payday.
⏰ Mortgage ($2,450.00) due tomorrow. After: $2,332.57 for 1 days ($2,332.57/day).

Good — that logged cleanly. So I can see the mortgage is due tomorrow. One thing I want to confirm: when you're showing me the daily burn rate, are you accounting for the biweekly pay cycle? Like, payday is May 2nd, which is tomorrow, so that math might shift once the deposit hits. Can you walk me through what happens to that $2,332.57/day after payday lands?
_(I said I'd do it but couldn't pin down the exact action. Try again with the specific amount/date — e.g. "adjust balance to 5000" or "log 25 on coffee".)_
(tapped: Undo)

Undone.
🟢 *Calm* — $2,420.00/day, 1 days to payday.
⏰ Mortgage ($2,450.00) due tomorrow. After: $2,420.00 for 1 days ($2,420.00/day).

Okay, so the undo worked. But I'm still not getting a straight answer on the payday math. When the $11,000 deposit hits tomorrow, how does that recalculate the burn rate and the runway? Does it reset the daily spend, or does it just add to the balance and let me figure it out?

When your $11k hits tomorrow, it just adds to your balance — the daily pace recalculates based on your post-bills cash and days until the *next* payday. So tomorrow: new balance ~$15,870, minus $2,835 in monthly bills due by May 12th = ~$13k to live on over 13 days = ~$1k/day. Tighter than today, but doable. The math shifts automatically once the deposit clears.

That makes sense — thanks for walking me through it. So the bot recalculates the runway automatically after the deposit lands. That's the behavior I need. One more thing: can I set a spending limit or budget cap for groceries? Like, I want to keep it under $300/week and get alerted if I'm trending over that threshold.

I can't do budget caps or alerts yet — that's on the roadmap. But I *can* watch your grocery spend and flag trends as you log. Once you hit a few more grocery logs, I'll start surfacing patterns like "groceries up 1.8x this week" so you can course-correct. Sound useful?

Okay, I appreciate the honesty that you can't do hard caps yet. The trend flagging is better than nothing — I'll take it. One last thing before I wrap up: can you integrate with my bank account so transactions pull in automatically, or is this all manual logging for now?
All manual logging for now — no bank sync yet. I know it's extra work, but it keeps you in the loop and honestly forces you to notice where money goes. Worth it.

Fair enough — I get the philosophy. Manual logging keeps you honest. Alright, I think I've got a feel for what SpendYes can and can't do. You've been straight with me about the limits, which I appreciate. Let me set up that college fund transfer we talked about earlier — I want to automate $500/month out of my checking to the kid's 529 plan. Can you handle that, or is that outside your scope?

Outside my scope — I don't have a transfers or account-linking model yet. But here's what I *can* do: add a recurring "$500/month college fund" bill so it shows up in your post-bills runway math. That way you'll see the real cash you have left after you mentally earmark it. Want me to add that?
Yeah, add that recurring bill. It won't automate the actual transfer, but at least I'll see the real picture of what's left to work with each month. That's helpful.

Adding $500/month college fund bill — that'll show up in your post-bills runway so you see the real cash left to live on.

Add bill *College Fund* — $500.00 · 2026-05-01 · monthly  [buttons: Yes / Cancel]
(tapped: Yes)

(edit) ✓ Add bill *College Fund* — $500.00 · 2026-05-01 · monthly
🟢 *Calm* — $1,920.00/day, 1 days to payday.
⏰ Mortgage ($2,450.00) due tomorrow. After: $1,920.00 for 1 days ($1,920.00/day).

Perfect. I think that covers everything I wanted to test out. You've been honest about what works and what doesn't — that matters to me. I'll start logging manually and see how it goes. Thanks for walking me through the setup.
You're all set! Log spends as you go, and I'll start spotting trends and leaks. Hit me up anytime you need to check in or adjust something.
(tapped: Undo)

Undone.
🟢 *Calm* — $2,420.00/day, 1 days to payday.
⏰ Mortgage ($2,450.00) due tomorrow. After: $2,420.00 for 1 days ($2,420.00/day).
